title: Fix panic! violations in apply.rs tests
description:
Replace panic!() calls in test assertions with assert!(matches!(...)) pattern.

**Locations (3 violations):**
- src/apply.rs:256 - test_apply_init_not_rust_crate
- src/apply.rs:276 - test_apply_init_not_git_repo
- src/apply.rs:300 - test_apply_init_conflicts_without_force

**Pattern to fix:**
Replace:
```rust
match result.unwrap_err() {
    ExpectedError => {}
    e => panic!("Expected ExpectedError, got: {:?}", e),
}
```

With:
```rust
assert!(
    matches!(result.unwrap_err(), ExpectedError),
    "Expected ExpectedError, got: {:?}", result
);
```

Or use assert_matches! macro if available.

- STYLE_GUIDE.md

- All 3 panic! calls in apply.rs tests are replaced

title: Decide whether ratchets config should enforce the full prose panic ban
description:
Surfaced by the Coding Subagent during bd-26x, and confirmed by the Judge. Out of scope for that bead (which documented behaviour without changing it).

## Measured gap

RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md bans unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in ALL code including tests, and the same intent covers unreachable!()/todo!()/unimplemented!(). The enabled ratchets enforce strictly less than that:

| Construct | src/** non-test | #[cfg(test)] in src/** | top-level tests/** |
|---|---|---|---|
| .unwrap()        | flagged     | flagged     | NOT (excluded) |
| .expect(...)     | NOT         | NOT         | NOT |
| panic!(...)      | flagged     | flagged     | NOT (excluded) |
| unreachable!()   | NOT         | NOT         | NOT |
| todo!()          | NOT by no-panic (text trips no-todo-comments) | same | same |
| unimplemented!() | NOT         | NOT         | NOT |

Cause: templates/ratchets.toml.liquid sets enabled_ratchets = ["$common-starter", "no-unwrap", "no-panic"]. A builtin 'no-expect' rule EXISTS in ratchets 0.3.1 but is not enabled. no-unwrap and no-panic both carry default exclude globs **/tests/** and **/benches/**. No builtin rule covers unreachable!/todo!/unimplemented!.

## Decision required (this is a policy question, not a mechanical fix)

1. Enable 'no-expect'. This will surface pre-existing .expect(...) call sites; a budget in ratchet-counts.toml for PRE-EXISTING violations is legitimate (per coordinator.md, a threshold may only ever absorb pre-existing violations being formally tracked, never a violation the current bead introduced). Measure the count first.
2. Override the default excludes so **/tests/** and **/benches/** are scanned. Same budget consideration.
3. Decide what to do about unreachable!/todo!/unimplemented!, for which no builtin rule exists. Options: a custom rule, or accept that these remain prose-only and rely on review.
4. Or: consciously accept the gap, and rely on the 'necessary but not sufficient' note added by bd-26x.

Whichever is chosen, the outcome must be recorded so the RUST_STYLE_GUIDE 'Ratchet rule scope' section stays accurate -- that section is now load-bearing and must be updated in the same commit as any config change.

## Files
- templates/ratchets.toml.liquid, ratchets.toml
- ratchet-counts.toml (budgets, pre-existing violations only)
- templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid + RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md (keep the scope note true)

WARNING: do not raise a budget to absorb a violation introduced by the same change. That is the routing-around-policy defect coordinator.md says to revert on sight.

Make coordinator commit guidance jj-aware in colocated repos
description:
Filed by the Coordinator from the 2026-07-09 Tidy pass (read-only; Tidy proposed, Coordinator filed).

## Problem

templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid was just made .jj/-aware for its ROLLBACK path at three sites (bead bd-wq1), and the document explicitly acknowledges the repo may be a colocated Jujutsu repo. But its COMMIT guidance is still git-only: the 'Commit-message escaping' section ends in a fenced block running 'git commit -F /tmp/commit-msg.txt'.

In a colocated jj repo the working copy is not staged by default, so a bare 'git commit -F <file>' commits nothing (or a partial index) rather than the working-copy change jj tracks. An agent that commits with git but rolls back with 'jj abandon' -- which this same document now instructs -- is driving two front-ends over one colocated store.

The document therefore contradicts itself about which VCS to drive. The Coordinator commits .beads/issues.jsonl through this path routinely.

## Prior art in this repo

The release-to-crates SKILL opens with 'Detect the environment first' and gates every VCS command on .jj/ existence, giving explicit jj and git variants. Follow that shape, and the shape bd-wq1 established for rollback.

## Required outcome

Rework the 'Commit-message escaping' section so committing branches on .jj/ detection exactly as the rollback guidance does: if .jj/ exists use jj desc / jj commit; otherwise the existing 'git commit -F <file>' path. Preserve the section's original point -- apostrophes in a bash heredoc break 'git commit -m' -- and note that the message-from-file idiom applies to the jj path too.

## Files
- templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid (source of truth)
- .claude/agents/coordinator.md (checked-in generated mirror; MUST change in the same commit -- currently byte-identical, and there is NO automated drift guard, see bd-3he)

Verification: commit guidance branches on .jj/ like the rollback guidance; template and mirror agree exactly.

title: Correct coordinator's claim about what the ratchets enforce
description:
Filed by the Coordinator from the 2026-07-09 Tidy pass. This inconsistency was REVEALED by bead bd-26x, which documented the ratchets' true scope.

## Problem

templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid, in the 'Policy rules are not negotiable' section, contains the bullet beginning:

  'If the repo bans constructs such as `.unwrap()` / `.expect(...)` / `panic!(...)` (enforced here by the `no-unwrap` / `no-panic` ratchets)...'

The parenthetical attributes enforcement of all three constructs to the no-unwrap/no-panic ratchets. That is false, and the newly-added 'Ratchet rule scope' section of RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md now says so explicitly (measured under ratchets 0.3.1, and independently reproduced by a Judge Subagent):
- no-unwrap flags .unwrap() only; it does NOT flag .expect(...). The builtin no-expect rule exists but is not enabled.
- no-panic flags the panic! macro only.

So on a literal reading coordinator.md claims the ratchets enforce .expect(...) when they do not. Two generated documents now disagree about what the tooling catches -- precisely the confusion the new style-guide section was written to dispel.

## Distinction from bd-16g

bd-16g asks whether the ratchets CONFIG should be changed to enforce the full prose ban. This bead does NOT touch config. It is a prose-accuracy fix: make coordinator.md describe the tooling correctly as it stands today. The two beads are independent; if bd-16g later changes the config, this prose must be updated again (as must the style guide's scope note).

## Required outcome

Reword the parenthetical so it no longer implies no-unwrap/no-panic flag .expect(...). State that the POLICY bans all three constructs while the RATCHETS catch only .unwrap() and panic!, and defer to the RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md 'Ratchet rule scope' section as the authority. That pointer already exists in the bullet; keep it. Do not weaken the policy statement itself -- the ban is absolute and includes test code.

## Files
- templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid (source of truth)
- .claude/agents/coordinator.md (checked-in generated mirror; MUST change in the same commit; no automated drift guard, see bd-3he)

Verification: coordinator prose no longer contradicts RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md 'Ratchet rule scope'; template and mirror agree exactly.

title: Fix trailing-prose indentation in coordinator known-staleness list
description:
Found by the Judge Subagent while reviewing bd-1tv; pre-existing, not introduced by that bead.

## Problem

In templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid (and its generated mirror .claude/agents/coordinator.md), the 'Known-staleness patterns' entry ends with two 2-space-indented sentences:
  'These shapes arise specifically during fallible-conversion and struct-changing refactors.'
  'When you see one of these immediately after a coding subagent's commit, run cargo check --all-targets once. ...'

Under CommonMark lazy continuation these fold into the final bullet's <li> rather than rendering as standalone paragraphs of the parent list item. Confirmed via pandoc. The pre-existing closing sentence was previously absorbed into the 'unresolved import' bullet for the same reason.

The list is not broken and the shapes render as separate items, so this is cosmetic. But the closing guidance applies to ALL the shapes, not just the last one, and it currently reads as trailing text of the E0560 bullet.

## Required outcome

Insert a blank line before each trailing prose sentence, keeping 2-space indentation, so both render as paragraphs of the parent list item rather than as continuation text of the last bullet. Verify the rendered HTML (e.g. pandoc) before and after. Apply to both the .liquid template and the checked-in mirror.

Verification: rendered HTML shows the closing guidance as a sibling paragraph of the bullet list, not inside the final <li>.

title: Document ratchet rule scope: no-panic/no-unwrap coverage is narrower than their names imply
description:
Migrated from downstream pristine bead bd-1ok.

The enabled ratchets (no-panic, no-unwrap, defined via `templates/ratchets.toml.liquid`) cover far less than their names suggest. This misled at least one downstream coding agent and risks future eradication campaigns declaring victory prematurely.

VERIFIED FACTS (from downstream pristine, whose ratchets.toml enables $common-starter + no-unwrap + no-panic, ratchet-counts all 0, `ratchets check` exits 0):
- no-panic flags `panic!(...)` ONLY under `src/`. It does NOT flag `panic!` in the top-level `tests/` directory, and does NOT flag `unreachable!()`/`todo!()`/`unimplemented!()` at all. A production `unreachable!()` and 9 `panic!` in `tests/` were un-flagged yet still needed removal per policy.
- no-unwrap passed at budget 0 even though `src/` contained 327 `.expect(...)` and at least one `.unwrap()`. So no-unwrap as configured does NOT flag `.expect(...)` and did not flag those `.unwrap()` forms. Its effective coverage must be characterized precisely.

ACTION: Add a short "Ratchet rule scope" note to `templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid` stating, per rule, exactly what the ratchet flags vs. what the prose policy bans. Make explicit that `ratchets check` passing is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for an "eradicate X" campaign: the human-readable policy ban (no panic-family macros anywhere, including `tests/` and `unreachable!`/`todo!`) is broader than the automated check. Investigate and record the actual no-unwrap matching behavior (`ratchets check -v` or the rule definition) so the doc is accurate, not assumed.

Note: the "verified facts" were measured on pristine; re-confirm against the current `templates/ratchets.toml.liquid` rule set before documenting, since rule definitions may have changed.

Verification: regenerated RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md contains a per-rule scope note and states that a green `ratchets check` is necessary-but-not-sufficient for eradication campaigns.
close_reason:
Judge PASS. 'Ratchet rule scope' section added to templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid and its generated mirror, stating that a green 'ratchets check' is necessary but not sufficient. All four documented claims were measured, not transcribed, and then independently reproduced by the Judge via a synthetic probe crate plus the installed ratchets-0.3.1 rule definitions: (a) no-unwrap flags .unwrap() but not .expect(...) (no-expect not enabled); (b) no-panic flags panic! but not unreachable!/todo!/unimplemented!; (c) both rules exclude **/tests/** and **/benches/**; (d) #[cfg(test)] modules inside src/ are NOT excluded and ARE flagged.

NOTE: this CONTRADICTS the bead's inherited 'VERIFIED FACTS', which were measured on downstream pristine and are stale. The bead claimed no-unwrap does not flag .unwrap(); in fact it does, in non-test src/ code. The committed prose documents the measured behaviour. No pristine-derived numbers leaked into the doc. ratchets.toml / ratchet-counts.toml unchanged (budgets still 0). Implemented in jj commit ltnzyvyn.

title: Generate .claude/skills -> .agents/skills symlink during apply
description:
rust-bucket generates OTHER repos, so the bridge symlink that lets Claude Code discover skills stored at the brand-agnostic .agents/skills/ location must be created by the generator (exactly like CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md), NOT hand-committed.

GOAL
Every applied repo gets a directory symlink .claude/skills -> ../.agents/skills, mirroring the existing create_claude_symlink pattern. This lets Claude Code (which discovers .claude/skills/) pick up skills whose canonical home is .agents/skills/.

WHAT TO DO (src/generator.rs)
1. Add pub fn create_skills_symlink(target_dir: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf, GeneratorError>, mirroring create_claude_symlink:
   - #[cfg(unix)]: link path = target_dir/.claude/skills ; target = "../.agents/skills" (relative, so it resolves correctly from inside .claude/). Ensure parent dir target_dir/.claude exists (create_dir_all) before linking - on a real apply it already exists from rendered agents, but the unit test must create it. Remove any existing file/symlink at the link path first (mirror the existing remove_file guard; use remove_file for a symlink, and handle a pre-existing real dir by remove_dir_all if needed). Use std::os::unix::fs::symlink (already imported).
   - #[cfg(windows)]: mirror the existing windows fallback. Since this is a directory, use std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir if available, else recursively copy .agents/skills -> .claude/skills. Match the style of the existing #[cfg(windows)] create_claude_symlink.
   - Return the link path (.claude/skills) as PathBuf.
2. Wire it into BOTH src/apply.rs flows, immediately AFTER the existing create_claude_symlink call:
   - apply_init (~step 8b) and apply_update (~step 10b): let skills_symlink = generator::create_skills_symlink(target_dir)?; files_generated.push(skills_symlink);
3. Update src/templates.rs managed_files():
   - Add "\.agents/skills/deburr-edge-cases/SKILL.md" (the generated skill output) and "\.claude/skills" (the symlink) to the vec.
   - Update the count assertion in tests (currently asserts len == 16) to the new length, and keep the other managed_files assertions valid.
4. Add a #[cfg(unix)] unit test in generator.rs tests for create_skills_symlink: create a temp dir, mkdir .claude, call the fn, assert the returned path is a symlink and its target is "../.agents/skills".

CONSTRAINTS
- Behaviour-preserving for all existing files; only adds the new symlink + managed_files entries.
- Keep diffs minimal; match surrounding code style and doc-comment conventions (see create_claude_symlink doc comment).

ACCEPTANCE
- cargo fmt --check passes.

title: Add a test asserting checked-in generated files match their rendered templates
description:
Found by the Judge Subagent while reviewing bd-1tv.

## Problem

This repo checks in both templates/*.liquid AND the top-level files generated from them (.claude/agents/coordinator.md, AGENTS.md, RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md, .config/nextest.toml, ...). The generated files carry a 'DO NOT EDIT BY HAND' banner and are expected to stay in sync with their source templates.

Nothing enforces this. The existing test test_version_stamp_in_generated_files only checks freshly-rendered output for version stamps; no test renders a .liquid source and asserts equality against the corresponding checked-in mirror. Source/mirror drift is therefore invisible to cargo nextest and ratchets check.

Every bead that edits a template depends on the coding subagent remembering to hand-update the mirror. bd-1tv did so correctly (Judge verified by diff), but that is a convention, not a guarantee. A subagent that edits only the template ships a repo whose generated docs silently disagree with their source.

## Required outcome

Add a test that, for every managed generated file, renders its .liquid template with this repo's own rust-bucket config and asserts the result equals the checked-in file. See src/templates.rs (the managed-file list, ~line 86), src/generator.rs, and rust-bucket.toml.

Design question for the implementer: the render needs the same config/context that 'rust-bucket apply' uses on this repo (README states rust-bucket must be able to apply its own repository without special cases). Reuse that path rather than reimplementing it.

Verification: mutating a checked-in generated file without its template (or vice versa) makes cargo nextest run fail.

title: Reconcile RUST_STYLE_GUIDE unwrap-in-tests prose with the absolute no-panic/no-unwrap ban
description:
Migrated from downstream pristine bead bd-3k5.

The `templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid` "Error handling (no panics)" section states that `unwrap()`/`expect()` "may be acceptable" in tests when they improve readability/failure messages. This contradicts the absolute policy the agent templates enforce:
- `templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid` "Policy rules are not negotiable": the ban on `.unwrap()`/`.expect()`/`panic!()` is absolute and INCLUDES unit tests; the only exception is a test asserting a panic.
- `templates/.claude/agents/coding.md.liquid` "Conform to lint policy - including in test code": same absolute stance, with the prescribed Result-returning test idiom.

Because RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md is generated from this template into every downstream repo, the contradiction propagates: a coding agent following the style guide (correctly) may add unwrap/expect in tests, which policy (enforced elsewhere) then rejects. Downstream repo `pristine` hit exactly this ambiguity.

ACTION: Edit `templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid`'s "Error handling (no panics)" section so the test-code stance matches the absolute ban: state that unwrap/expect/panic-family macros are banned in test code too, with the single `#[should_panic]` exception, and point to the Result-returning test idiom. Remove or correct the "may be acceptable in tests" sentence. Docs/template-only edit.

Verification: regenerated RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md no longer says unwrap/expect may be acceptable in tests, and its stance matches coordinator.md/coding.md.
close_reason: Already resolved upstream by commit ef15677 (Reconcile contradictory rust-bucket documentation and templates): templates/RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid 'Error handling (no panics)' no longer says unwrap/expect may be acceptable in tests. It now reads 'No unwrap(), expect(), or panic!() in any code, including tests', matching coordinator.md and coding.md. Verified by grep for 'may be acceptable' across templates/.

title: Fix invalid br create --id recipe in tidy agent template
description:
Found during the 2026-07-08 pristine de-complect tidy pass; the Coordinator hit the invalid command while filing a tidy follow-up bead.

## Problem

`templates/.claude/agents/tidy.md.liquid` instructs the Tidy agent to file beads with `br create --id "$(openssl rand -hex 3)"` (supply a randomly generated 6-char UUID as the bead ID). Two defects:

1. `br create` (beads_rust) has NO `--id` flag. The command fails outright ("For more information, try '--help'"). Only `br create --parent <ID>` takes an ID; the issue's own ID is always auto-generated.
2. It contradicts `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid` (and the generated CLAUDE.md), which state: "`br create` auto-generates short random IDs (e.g. `code-9y0`). Do not override them."

So the tidy template both prescribes a non-existent flag and violates the repo's own ID policy. Any Tidy agent following it literally errors; one that works around it may override IDs against policy.

## Required outcome

Edit `templates/.claude/agents/tidy.md.liquid`:
- Remove the `--id "$(openssl rand -hex 3)"` instruction.
- State that `br create` auto-generates the ID and must not be overridden, consistent with AGENTS.md.
- Preserve the surrounding "beads that age well" guidance.

Check the other agent templates (`coordinator.md.liquid`, `coding.md.liquid`, `reflection.md.liquid`, `judge.md.liquid`) for the same stale `br create --id` recipe and fix any occurrence.

Verification: no agent template instructs `br create --id`, and the ID-generation guidance is consistent across AGENTS.md and every agent template.
close_reason: Already resolved upstream by commit ef15677 (Reconcile contradictory rust-bucket documentation and templates): templates/.claude/agents/tidy.md.liquid no longer contains 'br create --id'/openssl rand; it now states that br create auto-generates IDs and they must not be overridden. No agent template instructs --id. Verified by grep across templates/ and git log -S 'openssl rand'.

title: Surface migrations in apply and enforce forward-only upgrades
description:
Wire the automatic half of the migration pathway into `apply`, and enforce forward-only upgrades. Reuse the migration renderer in src/migrations.rs (a function rendering `&[Migration]` to a String, plus the NO_MIGRATIONS_MESSAGE constant) added by the show-migration work.

1) Forward-only enforcement (src/apply.rs, apply_update):
- Before mutating anything (before Config::save / render), parse the recorded rust_bucket_version from rust-bucket.toml and the current binary version (env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")) as semver.
- If the binary version is OLDER than the recorded version, return a new typed ApplyError variant (e.g. DowngradeNotSupported { recorded, binary }) WITHOUT changing the stamp or regenerating files. main.rs prints it to STDERR and exits non-zero.

2) Surface migrations (src/apply.rs):
- In apply_update, capture the OLD recorded version before bumping the stamp; after the new (binary) version is known, compute migrations::migrations_between(&old, &new).
- Add fields to ApplyResult: `migrations: Vec<Migration>` and `old_version: Option<String>`. apply_init sets migrations empty and old_version None (first-time init has no prior version).
- Leave the existing regeneration + verification behavior otherwise unchanged. Migrations are purely informational: do NOT change how the process exit code is computed (still reflects verification only).

3) Footer output (src/main.rs, print_results):
- After the existing verification summary, ALWAYS print a migration footer (one unified path):
  - result.migrations non-empty: print the rendered guide markdown (reuse the migrations renderer) to STDOUT.
  - else: print NO_MIGRATIONS_MESSAGE to STDOUT.
- Then, when old_version is Some, print a re-view hint to STDOUT: `To view these again, run: rust-bucket show-migration --from <old_version>`. Omit the hint line when old_version is None (first-time init). This resolves the plan's open question about the init hint.
- The footer must NOT change the process exit code (still driven by verification success).

Tests (must land): apply_update returns DowngradeNotSupported when binary < recorded (write a rust-bucket.toml stamped with a high version such as 999.0.0); apply_update populates ApplyResult.migrations on a forward bump (stamp e.g. 0.5.0 so the embedded 0.6.0 guide is in range) and sets old_version Some; apply_init yields empty migrations and old_version None. Existing apply tests and tests/integration.rs must still pass. Use the fallible test style (no unwrap/expect/panic).

Out of scope: do NOT modify src/show_migration.rs or src/cli.rs. Do NOT delete src/upgrade.rs (separate bead). Do NOT stage or commit anything under .beads/. Standing rules in AGENTS.md and .claude/agents/coding.md apply.

title: Retire seed_style_guide; fold STYLE_GUIDE.md into the seed_files registry
description:
Goal: eliminate the bespoke STYLE_GUIDE.md seeding path and make STYLE_GUIDE.md a regular seed file handled by the unified `seed_files` mechanism, with pure seed-if-absent semantics (NEVER overwrite). This removes the two-parallel-mechanisms entropy: after this bead there is ONE seed path (`generator::seed_files` + `templates::seed_files()`).

Current state:
- `src/generator.rs`: `const STYLE_GUIDE_SEED` (line ~228) and `pub fn seed_style_guide` (line ~235). seed_style_guide has hybrid semantics: writes if absent, AND overwrites an existing file that still contains the `<!-- Generated by rust-bucket` header (stale-refresh), but leaves user-edited files alone.
- `src/apply.rs`: calls `generator::seed_style_guide(target_dir)?` in BOTH apply_init (~line 112) and apply_update (~line 175).
- `generator::seed_files` (line ~257) is the new generic seed path: pure seed-if-absent (writes only when destination does not exist), rendering an embedded template via liquid. `templates::seed_files()` (src/templates.rs:107) currently registers only `("ratchets.toml.liquid", "ratchets.toml")`.

Required changes (must-land):
1. Add `templates/STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid` containing exactly the current STYLE_GUIDE_SEED text (the "# Style Guide\n\nProject-specific coding standards go here.\nSee also `RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md`..." stub). NO "Generated by / DO NOT EDIT" header — seed files carry no header (match ratchets.toml.liquid style). It has no liquid variables; that is fine.
2. Register `("STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid", "STYLE_GUIDE.md")` in `templates::seed_files()`.
3. DELETE `STYLE_GUIDE_SEED` const and `seed_style_guide` fn from src/generator.rs. Remove BOTH `seed_style_guide` calls from src/apply.rs (the `seed_files` call already in both apply flows now covers STYLE_GUIDE.md). Ensure `files_generated` still reflects seeded paths.
4. **Resolve the semantic difference by simplification (per user decision): STYLE_GUIDE.md now matches the new seed-document semantics identically -- seed if absent, REFUSE to overwrite ever.** The old stale-refresh-by-header behavior is intentionally dropped. Do NOT add a refresh/staleness flag to seed_files.
5. STYLE_GUIDE.md must NOT be added to `templates::managed_files()` (stays 16). Confirm `render()` skips it because it is registered in `seed_files()` (render already skips seed templates in both passes).
6. Tests: delete/replace any tests for seed_style_guide; add coverage that STYLE_GUIDE.md is seeded when absent and left byte-for-byte unchanged when present. New test code must be unwrap-free (use `-> Result<...>` + `?`); the `no-unwrap` ratchet budget is at its 83 cap and must NOT be raised. Do NOT edit `ratchet-counts.toml`.

Verification / Definition of Done: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings), `cargo nextest run`, AND `ratchets check` (no-unwrap <= 83, no-panic 0) all pass. Self-hosting must stay safe: this repo already has STYLE_GUIDE.md, so seeding skips it.

Out of scope: do NOT touch docs (ARCHITECTURE.md/README.md) -- a separate bead covers that. Do NOT touch `src/verify.rs`. Do NOT delete `templates/cargo-generate.toml` -- separate bead.

Relevant files: src/generator.rs (seed_style_guide, STYLE_GUIDE_SEED, seed_files, render), src/templates.rs (seed_files, managed_files), src/apply.rs (apply_init/apply_update), templates/ratchets.toml.liquid (style reference for the new seed template). Standing rules in AGENTS.md and .claude/agents/coding.md apply.

title: Make capability-gated test skips visible under nextest default capture
description:
Migrated from downstream pristine bead bd-2csq (resolution routed to rust-bucket per maintainer decision: fix via the generated nextest/TESTING templates, not via downstream test code).

## Problem

A common test pattern is a runtime capability probe: when a precondition cannot be exercised in the current environment (e.g. the process can read a `0o000` file because it runs as root or holds `CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE`), the test `eprintln!`s a skip notice and returns early instead of asserting. Downstream `pristine` has two such sites (`test_support::deny_reads_or_skip`, `test_support::anthropic_key_or_skip`).

nextest's DEFAULT capture suppresses stderr from PASSING tests. The generated `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid` sets only `global-timeout`, no `success-output`. So a runner of plain `cargo nextest run` sees green tests and does NOT see the skip notice unless they pass `--no-capture` or `--success-output=immediate`. The notice is emitted but unread -- a test can silently assert nothing.

This bites any repo generated from these templates whose CI runs as root (as the generated devcontainer does: `templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.liquid` has no USER directive). A static `#[ignore]` cannot substitute, because the condition is a runtime capability check.

## Options (pick one, justify)

1. Set `success-output = "immediate"` (or "final") in `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid` so skip notices from passing tests surface. Assess the noise cost: this makes ALL passing-test stdout/stderr visible, not just skips.
2. Establish a documented convention in `templates/TESTING.md.liquid`: capability-gated skips are run under `--success-output=immediate` in CI, with the invocation recorded there. Lower blast radius, no behavior change for local `cargo nextest run`.
3. (Investigated and de-scoped for rust-bucket.) A downstream fix that changes the eprintln-then-pass shape in a repo's own `src/test_support.rs` -- pristine code, out of scope for the templates. If rust-bucket concludes only option 3 is viable (nextest has no runtime-skip mechanism that surfaces in the skipped count), bounce this back to a downstream pristine bead rather than editing templates.

## Scope
Whichever managed-file option is chosen, ensure the convention is documented so downstream repos apply it consistently to all capability-gated skip sites.

## Files (rust-bucket)
- templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid
- templates/TESTING.md.liquid

Verification: a repo generated from the updated templates surfaces a passing test's skip notice under the documented/default invocation, or TESTING.md records the exact CI invocation that does.
notes:
RESEARCH 2026-07-09 (Coordinator, web + empirical):

NO RUNTIME-SKIP MECHANISM EXISTS. The 'option 4' hypothesis is refuted:
- rust-lang/rust#68007 (ignore tests at runtime) is still OPEN, unimplemented since 2020-01-08. libtest only distinguishes panic (fail) from no-panic (pass); a test cannot report SKIPPED at runtime.
- nextest-rs/nextest#1532 (conditional skip via setup scripts) is CLOSED, but the timeline shows it was 'converted_to_discussion' and locked, NOT implemented. Maintainer (sunshowers) proposed adding a third 'skip' state to setup scripts and explicitly deferred: 'I don't imagine I'll get to this soon.'
- nexte.st setup-scripts docs confirm scripts are pass/fail only; a non-zero exit terminates the whole run. No per-test skip.
- nextest's status-level 'skip' counts only statically-skipped tests (#[ignore], filtersets), not runtime early-returns.
Therefore bead option 3's premise holds, and the eprintln-then-pass shape cannot be converted into a real SKIP by any template change.

BETTER THAN OPTION 1: success-output is settable PER-TEST via a filterset override, not just globally:
  [[profile.default.overrides]]
  filter = 'test(/_or_skip$/)'
  success-output = "immediate"
Verified empirically: this config parses and runs under the installed cargo-nextest 0.9.133 (probe via --config-file; exit 0). Supported override keys per nexte.st per-test-overrides docs include success-output/failure-output.
This gives option 1's zero-invocation visibility with option 2's blast radius, at the cost of requiring a NAMING CONVENTION on capability-gated tests (the filterset matches test names, not helper fn names).

RECOMMENDATION: combine scoped override + documented convention. Edit templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid to add a commented-out (or active) per-test override keyed to a documented name marker, and document in templates/TESTING.md.liquid: (a) the naming convention for capability-gated tests, (b) that a runtime skip reports as PASS and never as SKIP, so the notice is the only signal, (c) the CI invocation --success-output=immediate as a belt-and-braces fallback. Do NOT set a global success-output.

OPEN DESIGN QUESTION for the implementing bead: choose the name marker and decide whether the template ships the override active or commented. Downstream pristine sites are deny_reads_or_skip / anthropic_key_or_skip (helpers), so the marker must land on test fn names.

title: Add seed-file mechanism and seed ratchets.toml into target projects
description:
Goal: make every project that runs `rust-bucket apply` receive a ratchets.toml config, seeded ONCE and never overwritten on re-apply. This is how ratchets (imbue-ai/ratchets) becomes a dependency of all rust-bucket projects. The ratchets BINARY is already installed by templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.liquid; judge.md.liquid already references `ratchets check`. The only missing piece is the config file.

Concept: introduce "seed files" as a named category distinct from "managed files". Managed files (templates::managed_files) are overwritten unconditionally on every apply. Seed files are written ONLY if absent in the target, and never touched again once present — the project owns them. The existing generator::seed_style_guide (src/generator.rs:212) is the prototype of this category; STYLE_GUIDE.md is already a seed file in spirit.

Scope (must-land):
1. Add templates/ratchets.toml.liquid containing exactly the content of this repo's own ./ratchets.toml (the enabled_ratchets / [ratchets] / [output] config). Do NOT add a "Generated by rust-bucket / DO NOT EDIT BY HAND" header — seed files are meant to be customized by the project. It has no liquid variables; that is fine.
2. Add a seed-file registry + seeding helper in src/generator.rs (and/or src/templates.rs) that renders/copies a seed template into the target ONLY if the target path does not already exist. Semantics for ratchets.toml: seed iff the file is absent; if present, do nothing (no header/staleness check needed — pure seed-if-missing).
3. Wire the new seed step into BOTH apply_init and apply_update (src/apply.rs), alongside the existing seed_style_guide call.
4. ratchets.toml MUST NOT be added to templates::managed_files() (it must never be overwritten). Confirm check_conflicts (managed-files based) does not treat it as a conflict, so init does not refuse when a project already has a ratchets.toml.

title: Add show-migration subcommand and reusable migration renderer
description:
Wire the on-demand half of the migration pathway: a new `rust-bucket show-migration` command that prints embedded migration guides for a version range. Display-only — no file generation, no verification.

1) Reusable renderer (src/migrations.rs), which a later apply-footer task will also use:
- Add a pure function that renders a `&[Migration]` into one stdout-ready String: each migration's `instructions` concatenated in ascending version order, separated by a blank line.
- Add a public `&str` constant NO_MIGRATIONS_MESSAGE = "No upgrade instructions".

2) Command (src/cli.rs): add `ShowMigration { from: Option<String>, to: Option<String> }` to the `Commands` enum (clap derive) with `--from` / `--to` long flags.

3) New module src/show_migration.rs (non-mod-rs layout; add `pub mod show_migration;` to src/lib.rs). Add a function taking the target dir and optional from/to strings, returning a typed outcome (either rendered guide text, or a "no migrations" marker) and a typed thiserror error enum (mirror the style of src/upgrade.rs / src/apply.rs):
- Resolve `to`: --to if given, else env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").
- Resolve `from`: --from if given, else load rust-bucket.toml (Config::load) in the target dir and use its rust_bucket_version. If --from is omitted AND rust-bucket.toml is absent, return an error (not initialized; tell the user to pass --from/--to or run inside a rust-bucket repo).
- Parse both as full semver via semver::Version::parse. A non-semver string ("1", "v5", "0.9") is an error.
- If from > to, return an error. If from == to, the range is empty (NOT an error).
- Call migrations::migrations_between(&from, &to). Empty => "no migrations" outcome; non-empty => rendered-text outcome.

4) Dispatch (src/main.rs) for Commands::ShowMigration:
- rendered-text outcome: print guide markdown to STDOUT, exit 0.
- "no migrations" outcome: print NO_MIGRATIONS_MESSAGE to STDOUT, exit 0.
- error: route through main's existing Err path so it prints to STDERR and exits non-zero.

Stream/exit contract that MUST hold: guide text and the "No upgrade instructions" line go to STDOUT with exit 0; all true errors (unparseable version, from > to, not-initialized) go to STDERR with a non-zero exit.

Tests (must land): unit tests for the renderer (order, blank-line separation, empty constant); a clap parse test for `show-migration` with and without flags; resolution/validation tests: default-from-config, both-flags-work-without-config, from==to empty, from>to error, unparseable-version error, missing-config-without-from error. Use the existing fallible test style (`-> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>`, `?`, `ok_or`, assert_*); no unwrap/expect/panic (clippy bans them, including in tests).

Out of scope: do NOT modify src/apply.rs or src/upgrade.rs. Do NOT stage or commit anything under .beads/. Standing rules in AGENTS.md and .claude/agents/coding.md apply.

title: Make coordinator rollback guidance VCS-aware (git reset corrupts colocated jj repos)
description:
RESCOPED by the Coordinator on 2026-07-09 after verifying the current templates.

## Status of the original report

The original bead (migrated from downstream pristine) claimed templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid prescribes an UNCONDITIONAL 'git reset --hard' in three places. That is no longer true. Commit ef15677 ('Reconcile contradictory rust-bucket documentation and templates') added a jj alternative at all three sites:
- line 37 (If Judge fails):  'Run `git reset --hard` (or `jj abandon` the failed change in a Jujutsu repo) to revert to the pre-attempt commit.'
- line 63 (If a subagent fails):  'hard reset to pre-attempt commit: `git reset --hard <good_commit>` (or `jj abandon <failed_change>` in a Jujutsu repo)'
- line 137 (Graphviz RESET node):  'git reset --hard / jj abandon'

That corresponds to the original bead's third listed option ('state both the git and jj commands with a guard').

## Remaining gap

The guard is absent. All three sites present BOTH commands and leave the choice to the reader's judgement -- 'in a Jujutsu repo' never says how to determine whether this IS one. An agent that does not already know it is in a colocated repo will reach for the git command, which is the leading and more familiar one. In a colocated repo, 'git reset --hard' desyncs the jj working-copy commit from git HEAD and can lose work.

Note the failure is asymmetric: git-in-a-jj-repo corrupts state, while jj-in-a-git-repo merely fails to run. The guidance should therefore make the jj path the one an agent falls into by default when the repo is colocated, not the one it must know to look for.

## Required outcome

Make the guard explicit and mechanical at all three sites: the agent should test for the presence of '.jj/' and choose on that basis, e.g. 'if `.jj/` exists, use `jj abandon <change>`; otherwise `git reset --hard <good_commit>`.' Wording is the implementer's; the detection step must be stated, not implied.

Keep the diff to those three sites. Do NOT restructure the surrounding sections.

## Files
- templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid (source of truth)
- .claude/agents/coordinator.md (checked-in generated mirror -- MUST be updated in the same commit)

Also check templates/.claude/agents/coding.md.liquid for any 'git reset' rollback reference; at the time of rescoping, `grep -rn 'git reset' templates/` matched ONLY coordinator.md.liquid, so no mirror edit is expected there. Re-verify rather than assume.

Verification: no site instructs 'git reset --hard' without a stated .jj/-detection step, and the template and its generated mirror agree exactly.
close_reason: Judge PASS (rescoped). All three rollback sites in templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid and its generated mirror now state a mechanical .jj/-existence check, leading with the jj path so a colocated repo routes to 'jj abandon' by default and 'git reset --hard' is the explicit else-branch. Judge verified DOT validity of the RESET node by running graphviz dot -Tcanon/-Tsvg (not by inspection), confirmed template/mirror identical by diff, and re-ran fmt/clippy/nextest(69)/ratchets green. Implemented in jj commit znpyptok.

title: Gitignore and untrack .beads/last-touched in template and full
description:
GOAL
Stop .beads/last-touched from being version-tracked (it is rewritten by br on every command, so it perpetually shows dirty). Remove it from tracking and gitignore it, in BOTH the generator template path AND this repo's own (full/self-applied) .gitignore.

BACKGROUND
- This repo is the generator. The .gitignore is NOT a template file; required ignore lines come from templates::required_gitignore_lines() in src/templates.rs (currently lines 73-75: .beads/.br_history/, .beads/beads.db, .beads/beads.db-wal). generator::ensure_gitignore() (src/generator.rs:186) APPENDS any missing required lines to the target .gitignore (it does not regenerate; that is why the hand-added .beads/.write.lock coexists).
- This is a colocated Jujutsu + git repo (.jj present, HEAD detached). Use jj for VCS, not raw git, to avoid the earlier reconciliation footgun.

CHANGES
1. src/templates.rs: add "\".beads/last-touched\"," to the required_gitignore_lines() vec (keep it grouped with the other .beads/ entries; ordering not critical but keep tidy).
2. src/generator.rs tests that assert exact gitignore content must be updated to stay green:
   - test_ensure_gitignore_creates_file_when_missing (~line 638): added.len() goes 3 -> 4.
   - test_ensure_gitignore_appends_missing_lines (~line 650): added.len() goes 3 -> 4.
   - test_ensure_gitignore_skips_existing_lines (~line 659-667): the pre-seeded .gitignore string literal (line 663) must also include ".beads/last-touched\n" so that ensure_gitignore returns empty (added.is_empty()). Add it to the seeded content.
   - Run the full suite; fix any other gitignore assertions that count or match exact lines.
3. Regenerate this repo's own .gitignore via the project's self-apply path so .beads/last-touched is appended to the root .gitignore ("full" side). FOOTGUN: templates/ is embedded via rust-embed with no rerun-if-changed, so you MUST `cargo clean -p rust-bucket-cli && cargo build` before running `./target/debug/rust-bucket apply`, or the apply will use stale embedded data. (Note: this particular change is in src, not templates/, so a normal rebuild picks it up — but rebuild before apply regardless.) Verify the root .gitignore now contains .beads/last-touched under the beads section.
4. Untrack the file (the "remove" step). Because it must be ignored before jj will drop it: ensure .gitignore already lists it (step 3), then run `jj file untrack .beads/last-touched`. Confirm with `jj st` that .beads/last-touched no longer appears and is not tracked (`jj file list | grep last-touched` returns nothing). Do NOT delete the file from disk (br will recreate it); just stop tracking it.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Do NOT recreate the previously-lost bead (bd-lrv) or touch .beads/issues.jsonl beyond what the coordinator handles. Do NOT change .write.lock handling or other ignore lines. No unrelated refactors. No version re-stamp.

COMMIT
- One atomic commit containing: src/templates.rs, src/generator.rs (tests), and the root .gitignore change, plus the untrack of .beads/last-touched. Commit via jj (describe the working copy, then `jj new`). Leave .beads/issues.jsonl OUT of this commit (the coordinator owns bead-state commits) — if br/self-apply caused issues.jsonl or last-touched data churn, `jj restore` those before committing so they are not bundled in.
- Commit subject: capitalized imperative verb, no trailing period, no Conventional-Commits prefix. E.g. "Gitignore and untrack .beads/last-touched".

DEFINITION OF DONE
- cargo fmt --check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features passes (no warnings)
- cargo nextest run passes
- ratchets check passes
- Root .gitignore contains .beads/last-touched; required_gitignore_lines() includes it; file is untracked; jj st clean (aside from anything the coordinator will handle).
- Report exactly what you changed, the self-apply command used, and pass/fail of each DoD check.
